content: I just got back from an amazing sabbatical traveling through Eastern Europe. I visited 7 countries in 3 months and had the time of my life.
Wow, that sounds incredible! What was your favorite country or experience from the trip?
I think my favorite was Croatia. The food, the people, the scenery - everything was just so beautiful. I even got to try my hand at cooking some traditional Croatian dishes, like peka and paprika.
Yum, that sounds delicious! I've heard great things about Croatian cuisine. Did you travel alone or with someone?

I traveled with my partner, Rachel. We've been together for 10 years now, and it was great to have some quality time together. We're actually thinking of starting a travel blog to document our future adventures.
That's a great idea! You could share your recipes and travel tips with others. What inspired you to take a sabbatical, and how did you make it work with your job as an Interior Designer?

I've been feeling burnt out for a while, and I needed a break to recharge. I've been working with a great team, and they were really understanding about my need for time off. I've been with the company for 5 years now, and I feel lucky to have such a supportive workplace.
It's great that you prioritized your well-being and took the time you needed. Now that you're back, are you looking forward to any upcoming projects or events?

Actually, I've got a big project coming up - designing the interior of a new restaurant in Seattle. It's going to be a lot of work, but I'm excited for the challenge. And in my free time, I've been getting back into woodworking and home brewing beer.
That sounds like a great project! You must be really skilled to take on a commercial design project like that. Do you have any fun plans for the holidays?

Yeah, Rachel and I are planning to host a big dinner party for our friends and family. We're thinking of making some traditional Thai dishes, since that's one of my favorite cuisines. Maybe we'll even break out the chess board and have a tournament.
That sounds like a fantastic way to spend the holidays. I'm sure your guests will appreciate the delicious food and good company. Have you thought about what you'll do after the dinner party?
We're actually thinking of taking a weekend trip to the coast in January. We've got a friend who has a cabin in Cannon Beach, and we're planning to spend some time hiking and relaxing.
That sounds like a great way to unwind after the holidays. You'll have to let me know how it goes!
Will do. Thanks for chatting with me - it's been great to catch up and talk about my trip.
It was great talking to you too! I'm glad I could help you process your experiences and look forward to your future adventures.
